title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs rss 2.0, atom 1.0, namespaces, and extension policy from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for standards-aware syndication vocabulary map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Rss Channel Item · changed case · Review Feed Go Rss Channel Item
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 7832 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Extension Policy, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Extension Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Extension Policy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 5274 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state.
